page: Amazon Web Services is4gen.large is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture using the AWS Neoverse-N1 model running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 12.0 GB of system memory, providing a 6.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of a 1875 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for applications requiring high-capacity local storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance is optimized primarily for storage-heavy workloads rather than raw computational speed. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated as average, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the weak and poor categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with read-only large block speeds at 37.37 GB/sec and write speeds at 18.32 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware-accelerated AES instructions, though other advanced vector and matrix extensions are absent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the is4gen.large offers a high density of local NVMe storage relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for storage-intensive applications that do not require intensive CPU processing. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Appropriate workloads for this profile include local databases, distributed file systems, caching layers, and low-intensity data storage applications.
description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.large is an ARM64 storage-optimized server instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores. It is equipped with 12.0 GB of system memory and a substantial 1875 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results, indicating a design optimized for storage capacity over raw compute power. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy workloads that benefit from high local SSD capacity without requiring high-performance CPU scaling. It is best suited for local databases, caching layers, and distributed storage applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.large is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring 2 Graviton cores, 12 GB RAM, and a 1875 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.large is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance with 2 Graviton cores, 12 GB RAM, and a 1875 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring a high-capacity local NVMe SSD and dedicated Graviton processor cores for data-centric workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 12.0 GB of RAM and 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Average single-core CPU performance with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography
Optimized for local databases, caching layers, and distributed storage systems
